title: serpocaulon × manizalense
intro: Serpocaulon × manizalense is a hybrid species of fern, resulting from the cross between Serpocaulon polyphyllum and a related species in the Pteridaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Serpocaulon × manizalense include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and crushed, then used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
